alternatives.name: Ultrasonic transit-time flow sensor
alternatives.tradeoffs: ±1–3% accuracy (vs ±0.5% mag); degrades with air bubbles >2% vol; requires 5–10×DN straight pipe run; needs fluid-type configuration at commissioning; emerging awareness in aquaculture [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Electromagnetic (mag) flowmeter
alternatives.tradeoffs: 5–10× cost ($500–2,000+ vs $100–300 for inline ultrasonic); higher accuracy (±0.5%) but overkill for most farm-scale monitoring; larger footprint; higher power consumption (10–20 W); requires conductive liquid (met by default in freshwater RAS) [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Paddle-wheel / turbine flow sensor
alternatives.tradeoffs: Very low cost ($20–80); BUT clogs with biofilm in 2–6 weeks in RAS water; moving parts wear in particulate-laden water; contamination risk from bearing lubricant; no anomaly detection; ±2–5% accuracy drift; 45–55% incumbent share [SRC: VN_finfish_farming, web_search]

alternatives.name: Differential-pressure measurement (orifice/venturi)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Permanent pressure drop wastes pump energy (critical in energy-intensive RAS); fouling of sensing ports by biofilm; limited turndown ratio (3:1); requires DP transmitter; rarely used in aquaculture [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Do nothing / manual observation [Status Quo]
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero cost — 15–25% of farms; completely blind to flow failure; relies on downstream DO alarm or fish behavioral change; by the time operators notice, mortality may already be underway; historically acceptable on small ponds, catastrophic in RAS [SRC: VN_finfish_farming]
